canonical_subject_history.value: That exported encryption key is not a Matrix Client‑Server API access token.

units.text: That exported encryption key is not a Matrix Client‑Server API access token.
It can’t be used for `Authorization: Bearer …`, which is why the homeserver keeps returning `M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`.
Do this instead (locally in Element; don’t paste the token into chat):
Get a real access token:
Element Desktop: `Settings` → `Help & About` → `Access Token` → copy.
If you don’t see “Access Token”, tell me your Element platform (Desktop/macOS vs Web vs iOS/Android) and I’ll give the exact location for that build.
Fix `homeserver_url` if you’re on Matrix.org:
For matrix.org accounts, set `homeserver_url` to `https://matrix-client.matrix.org` (not `https://matrix.org`) in `config/cce.json`.
Edit `config/cce.json` safely:
`nano config/cce.json`
set `homeserver_url`, `user_id`, and `access_token` (no spaces/newlines, no `Bearer ` prefix)
save/exit
Once that’s done, say “updated” and I’ll rerun `whoami` and then list joined rooms with only `{room_id, room_name (if any), bot_joined}` so you can pick the right `room_id`.
Found exactly one joined room for the bot (no token printed):
`room_id`: `!RTrMYxXPsEMHvHuVqt:matrix.org`
`room_name`: `Matrix.org (Official Account)`
`bot_joined`: `true`
I updated `config/cce.json` to set that `room_id` (without exposing secrets) and ran the required config check:
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-config`: PASSED
Room verification result (no secrets involved):
`room_id`: `!RTrMYxXPsEMHvHuVqt:matrix.org`
`room_name`: `Matrix.org (Official Account)`
`join_rule`: `invite`
`history_visibility`: `shared`
This does not look like an intended private CCE test room; it looks like an “official account” room.
Also, it strongly suggests the bot is not currently joined to your private test room (otherwise it would have shown up as a joined room earlier).
I’m going to pause before running `--check-connection` / starting the listener, because we’d be verifying the wrong room.
If you want to proceed correctly, the fastest path is:
In Element, open your intended CCE test room → Room settings / Advanced → copy the **internal Room ID** (starts with `!…:domain`)
Tell me just that `room_id` (no token), and I’ll update `config/cce.json` locally and then continue with:
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection`
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json` and I’ll tell you when it’s waiting for `status`
If there are no joined rooms for the bot right now, creating a fresh private room is the cleanest way to get an unambiguous `room_id` without you having to hunt through Element’s UI.
Do this in Element (human steps, no secrets):
Create a new room named something unique, e.g.
`CCE Test Room (Phase 2.1)`.
Make it **Private** (invite-only).
Turn **off** public directory listing.
(Optional) Turn on E2EE; CCE doesn’t need to read encrypted content for `status`, but it’s fine either way for this phase.
Invite the bot user `@cce-matrix:matrix.org` to the room and wait until it shows as joined.
Once that’s done, tell me “room created + bot joined”, and I’ll:
Query Matrix for the bot’s `joined_rooms` using only the local `config/cce.json` token (not printed),
Show you candidates as `{room_id, room_name, bot_joined}`,
Update `config/cce.json` with the chosen `room_id` locally,
Then proceed with:
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection`
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json` and I’ll tell you when it’s waiting for you to send `status`.
I can’t see your new room under `joined_rooms` yet, but I *can* see pending invites in `/sync` (so the bot is invited, not joined).
Candidates (token not printed; these are safe to show):
`room_id`: `!MGpEaDEkYVrVwdFUoe:matrix.org` | room name: `CCE Test Room (Phase 2.1)` | bot joined: `false` (invited)
`room_id`: `!fUGhokKstimDiirFoo:matrix.org` | room name: `(none)` | bot joined: `false` (invited)
Which one is your intended private CCE test room?

If it’s the one named `CCE Test Room (Phase 2.1)`, reply `MGpEaDEkYVrVwdFUoe` (or paste the full `room_id`) and I’ll update `config/cce.json` and run `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-config` as requested (without exposing secrets).
`python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json` is running (session active).
Logs show:
`sync cursor state absent: runtime/matrix-sync-state.json`
`sync cursor initialized and saved: runtime/matrix-sync-state.json`
`initial sync ok; entering listener loop`
Runtime is now waiting for a live Element message.
Send `status` in `CCE Test Room (Phase 2.1)` and I’ll watch for `message received`, `reply sent`, cursor updates, and ensure there’s no own-message loop.

turns.answer:
That exported encryption key is not a Matrix Client‑Server API access token. It can’t be used for `Authorization: Bearer …`, which is why the homeserver keeps returning `M_UNKNOWN_TOKEN`.

Do this instead (locally in Element; don’t paste the token into chat):

1. Get a real access token:
- Element Desktop: `Settings` → `Help & About` → `Access Token` → copy.
- If you don’t see “Access Token”, tell me your Element platform (Desktop/macOS vs Web vs iOS/Android) and I’ll give the exact location for that build.

2. Fix `homeserver_url` if you’re on Matrix.org:
- For matrix.org accounts, set `homeserver_url` to `https://matrix-client.matrix.org` (not `https://matrix.org`) in `config/cce.json`.

3. Edit `config/cce.json` safely:
- `nano config/cce.json`
- set `homeserver_url`, `user_id`, and `access_token` (no spaces/newlines, no `Bearer ` prefix)
- save/exit

Once that’s done, say “updated” and I’ll rerun `whoami` and then list joined rooms with only `{room_id, room_name (if any), bot_joined}` so you can pick the right `room_id`.

turns.answer:
If there are no joined rooms for the bot right now, creating a fresh private room is the cleanest way to get an unambiguous `room_id` without you having to hunt through Element’s UI.

Do this in Element (human steps, no secrets):

1. Create a new room named something unique, e.g. `CCE Test Room (Phase 2.1)`.
- Make it **Private** (invite-only).
- Turn **off** public directory listing.
- (Optional) Turn on E2EE; CCE doesn’t need to read encrypted content for `status`, but it’s fine either way for this phase.

2. Invite the bot user `@cce-matrix:matrix.org` to the room and wait until it shows as joined.

Once that’s done, tell me “room created + bot joined”, and I’ll:
- Query Matrix for the bot’s `joined_rooms` using only the local `config/cce.json` token (not printed),
- Show you candidates as `{room_id, room_name, bot_joined}`,
- Update `config/cce.json` with the chosen `room_id` locally,
- Then proceed with:
  - `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json --check-connection`
  - `python3 -m gateway.runtime --config config/cce.json` and I’ll tell you when it’s waiting for you to send `status`.
